title: meconopsis paniculata subsp. paniculata
intro: Meconopsis paniculata subsp. paniculata, commonly known as the Himalayan blue poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It is native to the Himalayas and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Meconopsis paniculata subsp. paniculata include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and exhibiting potential antitumor activity. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the roots and leaves, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
